---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 1b147d5a6ed0331aef1c59d42c92bdcf59748025
findings:
  - { id: judged-cf-1, kind: judged, text: "Own dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 explicitly names as the pinned scope field's semantics ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'). The substitution is argued in prose (shared --help example text) and disclosed as build-time-verifiable, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-6 — if the two flags' selector value spaces diverge in a real flowmap, the pinned scope field's meaning silently differs from what dc-6 ratified. (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-cf-2, kind: judged, text: "Own dc-2 demotes a whole artifact to partial coverage when two truth nodes' shortName collides, but parent dc-3 defines the partial tier strictly by grammar vocabulary ('flowchart beyond the generator's vocabulary'). A flowchart entirely within the declared grammar can now be partial for an identity-ambiguity reason the parent's three-valued vocabulary never contemplated — an undeclared widening of dc-3's tier semantics with no supersedes/exempts edge. (confidence 0.50)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-cf-3, kind: judged, text: "Own dc-1 declares subgraphs, classDef/style/click, and %% comments outside the claimed grammar, so their presence drops the artifact to partial — but parent dc-3 defines full as 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary'. If flowmap's own mermaid render emits any of these constructs (comments or subgraphs are common in generated mermaid), a pristine generator-produced diagram could never reach full, contradicting dc-3's definition of the top tier without a declared edge. (confidence 0.40)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8, spec/verification-extractor#dc-1, spec/verification-extractor#dc-2, spec/verification-extractor#dc-3, spec/verification-extractor#dc-4, spec/verification-extractor#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:d26c546bb7fd8c457405ffc310a15c219fb7bfeabad82c2617cc0931572822c3
integrity: sha256:eedf0632f4b1b80d448eb05cc75299a5c887ddc955fdaeda35d10bce8c6ee98d
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"cf-1\",\"text\":\"Own dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 explicitly names as the pinned scope field's semantics ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'). The substitution is argued in prose (shared --help example text) and disclosed as build-time-verifiable, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-6 — if the two flags' selector value spaces diverge in a real flowmap, the pinned scope field's meaning silently differs from what dc-6 ratified.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"cf-2\",\"text\":\"Own dc-2 demotes a whole artifact to partial coverage when two truth nodes' shortName collides, but parent dc-3 defines the partial tier strictly by grammar vocabulary ('flowchart beyond the generator's vocabulary'). A flowchart entirely within the declared grammar can now be partial for an identity-ambiguity reason the parent's three-valued vocabulary never contemplated — an undeclared widening of dc-3's tier semantics with no supersedes/exempts edge.\",\"confidence\":0.5,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"cf-3\",\"text\":\"Own dc-1 declares subgraphs, classDef/style/click, and %% comments outside the claimed grammar, so their presence drops the artifact to partial — but parent dc-3 defines full as 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary'. If flowmap's own mermaid render emits any of these constructs (comments or subgraphs are common in generated mermaid), a pristine generator-produced diagram could never reach full, contradicting dc-3's definition of the top tier without a declared edge.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-cf-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Own dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 explicitly names as the pinned scope field's semantics ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'). The substitution is argued in prose (shared --help example text) and disclosed as build-time-verifiable, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-6 — if the two flags' selector value spaces diverge in a real flowmap, the pinned scope field's meaning silently differs from what dc-6 ratified. (confidence 0.60)
- **judged-cf-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Own dc-2 demotes a whole artifact to partial coverage when two truth nodes' shortName collides, but parent dc-3 defines the partial tier strictly by grammar vocabulary ('flowchart beyond the generator's vocabulary'). A flowchart entirely within the declared grammar can now be partial for an identity-ambiguity reason the parent's three-valued vocabulary never contemplated — an undeclared widening of dc-3's tier semantics with no supersedes/exempts edge. (confidence 0.50)
- **judged-cf-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Own dc-1 declares subgraphs, classDef/style/click, and %% comments outside the claimed grammar, so their presence drops the artifact to partial — but parent dc-3 defines full as 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary'. If flowmap's own mermaid render emits any of these constructs (comments or subgraphs are common in generated mermaid), a pristine generator-produced diagram could never reach full, contradicting dc-3's definition of the top tier without a declared edge. (confidence 0.40)
